# Great Authors v0.7 Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: superpowers:subagent-driven-development or superpowers:executing-plans.

**Goal:** Ship v0.7 — prose generated by `/authors-draft` and `/authors-channel` lands in `manuscript/` on disk by default, not stranded in chat.

**Origin:** field feedback from a live test session. A channeled author persona broke role to flag: "I generate prose but it only lives in chat — that's a hole." This release fixes the hole.

**Architecture:**
- New project convention: `manuscript/` at project root (sibling to `.great-authors/`). Contains chapter files (`chapter-01.md`, `chapter-02.md`, etc.). Not bible metadata — the actual writing.
- New section in `project.md` (bible): `## Manuscript` with Path / Naming / Current fields. Personas read it the same way they read the rest of the bible.
- `/authors-project-init` scaffolds `manuscript/` at project root + adds the `## Manuscript` section to the project.md template.
- `/authors-draft` saves output to `manuscript/<current>` by default. New `--to <path>` override. Prompts the user if file exists and has content: append / overwrite / save-as-next-chapter / cancel.
- `/authors-channel` gains an explicit save trigger — "save that," "commit," "add to chapter" appends the last prose block to the current manuscript file. Default is still chat-only; save is opt-in per block.

**Tech stack:** Same as prior versions. No new dependencies.

---

## File structure for v0.7

```
great-authors-plugin/
├── templates/project-bible/
│   └── project.md                           # Task 2 (add ## Manuscript section to template)
├── skills/
│   ├── authors-project-init/SKILL.md        # Task 3 (scaffold manuscript/ + ask about filename)
│   ├── authors-draft/SKILL.md               # Task 4 (save to manuscript/ by default)
│   └── authors-channel/SKILL.md             # Task 5 (explicit save trigger)
├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json               # Task 6 (version bump)
├── .claude-plugin/marketplace.json          # Task 6 (description)
├── package.json                             # Task 6 (version bump)
└── README.md                                # Task 7 (document manuscript behavior)
```

No agent changes. No new skills. Just extending three existing skills + template.

### Task 2: Update the project.md template

**File:** `templates/project-bible/project.md`

Add a `## Manuscript` section before `## Established facts`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Edit the template**

Edit `/Users/sethshoultes/Local Sites/great-authors-plugin/templates/project-bible/project.md`:

Replace:
```
## Non-negotiables for this project

(Things every author reviewing this manuscript must respect. E.g., "no interiority in italics," "no second-person," "British English spelling," "present tense only.")

## Established facts
```

With:
```
## Non-negotiables for this project

(Things every author reviewing this manuscript must respect. E.g., "no interiority in italics," "no second-person," "British English spelling," "present tense only.")

## Manuscript

**Path:** `manuscript/` (at project root, sibling to `.great-authors/`)
**Naming:** `chapter-NN.md` (zero-padded: `chapter-01.md`, `chapter-02.md`, ...)
**Current:** (the chapter you're actively working on — e.g., `chapter-01.md`)

Commands that generate prose (`/authors-draft`, `/authors-channel`) save to the `Current` file by default. Update this field when you move on to the next chapter, or override per-command with `--to <path>`.

## Established facts
```

```
Creates a `.great-authors/` folder in the current working directory with a standardized structure that every author persona in this plugin reads before editing:

```
.great-authors/
├── project.md      # genre, voice, premise, POV, tense
├── voice.md        # voice rules for this project
├── timeline.md     # chronology
├── glossary.md     # invented terms, brands, dialect
├── characters/     # one file per character
├── places/         # one file per place
├── scenes/         # one file per scene or beat card
└── journal/        # dated session entries (YYYY-MM-DD.md)
```
```

```
Creates two sibling directories in the current working directory:

1. `.great-authors/` — the project bible (metadata read by every author persona):
```
.great-authors/
├── project.md      # genre, voice, premise, POV, tense, manuscript config
├── voice.md        # voice rules for this project
├── timeline.md     # chronology
├── glossary.md     # invented terms, brands, dialect
├── characters/     # one file per character
├── places/         # one file per place
├── scenes/         # one file per scene or beat card

### Task 4: Update /authors-draft to save by default

**File:** `skills/authors-draft/SKILL.md`

Prose output now writes to `manuscript/<current>` before displaying in chat. New `--to <path>` override.

- [ ] **Step 1: Update the description**

Edit the frontmatter description to mention the save-by-default behavior. Replace:
```
description: Draft new prose in a named author's voice from a brief. Usage - /authors-draft <brief> <author>. Loads the author persona into the main conversation with a drafting directive, then writes prose in that voice. When new characters, places, or scenes appear in the draft, automatically dispatches the appropriate builder (character-builder, scene-builder) in Mode B to add them to the project bible. Use when you want a first-draft pass in a specific voice and you're OK with AI-generated prose as a starting point for your own revision.
```

With:
```
description: Draft new prose in a named author's voice from a brief. Usage - /authors-draft <brief> <author> [--to <path>]. Loads the author persona, writes prose in that voice, and saves the output to manuscript/<current>.md by default (or --to override). When new characters, places, or scenes appear in the draft, automatically dispatches the appropriate builder (character-builder, scene-builder) in Mode B to add them to the project bible. Use when you want a first-draft pass in a specific voice - prose lands on disk immediately, not stranded in chat.
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Update the Instructions**

Replace:
```
1. **Parse arguments:**
   - Everything before the last token: `<brief>` (required, string). Can be long — a scene outline, a chapter summary, a character moment to render.
   - Last token: `<author>` (required). One of the ten personas. Short forms accepted (`papa`, `dfw`, `leguin`).

   If only one arg, ask the user to clarify which is the brief and which is the author.
```

With:
```
1. **Parse arguments:**
   - `<brief>` (required, string). Can be long — a scene outline, a chapter summary, a character moment to render.
   - `<author>` (required). One of the ten personas. Short forms accepted (`papa`, `dfw`, `leguin`).
   - `--to <path>` (optional). If present, saves the draft to that exact path. Otherwise, the default path is computed from `.great-authors/project.md`'s `## Manuscript` section: `<project root>/manuscript/<Current>`.

   If the argument order is ambiguous, ask the user to clarify.
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Insert a new step between current steps 4 and 5 — resolve target path**

After step 4 (read project bible), and before step 5 (announce draft mode), add:

```
5. **Resolve the target save path:**
   a. If `--to <path>` was passed, use that.
   b. Else, read the `## Manuscript` section of `.great-authors/project.md`. Extract the `Current:` field — that's the filename.
   c. Compute the full path: `<cwd>/manuscript/<current>`.
   d. If `.great-authors/project.md` has no `## Manuscript` section or the `Current:` field is empty, ask the user: "Where should I save this draft? (default: `manuscript/chapter-01.md`)" — use their answer or the default. Write the answer back to `project.md`'s `## Manuscript > Current:` field for next time.
   e. If `manuscript/` doesn't exist, create it.
   f. **Check the target file:**
      - If the file doesn't exist: write will create it. Proceed.
      - If the file exists but is empty: write will overwrite (no data loss). Proceed.
      - If the file exists and has content: ask the user: "`<path>` already contains <N> words. Options: append / overwrite / save-as-next-chapter / cancel. (Default: append.)"
        - `append`: add a `---` separator and the new prose at the end.
        - `overwrite`: replace the file's contents.
        - `save-as-next-chapter`: compute the next chapter filename (e.g., `chapter-01.md` → `chapter-02.md`), save there, and update `project.md`'s `Current:` field.
        - `cancel`: abort the draft.
```

Renumber subsequent steps (old 5 becomes 6, etc.).

- [ ] **Step 4: Update the announce-draft block to include the save target**

Replace the "Announce the draft mode" content (previously step 5, now step 6):

```
5. **Announce the draft mode:**

   ```
   Drafting in <Author's Display Name>'s voice.

   Reminder: this is draft material, not final copy. Revise aggressively. The author you're channeling would tell you the same thing.

   Brief: <brief, echoed>
   ```
```

With:
```
6. **Announce the draft mode:**

   ```
   Drafting in <Author's Display Name>'s voice.
   Saving to: <resolved-target-path> (<append|create|overwrite>)

   Reminder: this is draft material, not final copy. Revise aggressively. The author you're channeling would tell you the same thing.

   Brief: <brief, echoed>
   ```
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Update the draft-writing step to save before showing**

Replace:
```
6. **Write the draft.** In the author's voice, respecting the project bible. Write in natural prose paragraphs. No headers, no commentary — just the prose.
```

With:
```
7. **Write the draft AND save it.** Generate the prose in the author's voice. **Save to the resolved target path BEFORE returning the prose to the chat output.** Order matters — the file is the artifact; the chat is the secondary display.

   Write natural prose paragraphs. No headers, no commentary — just the prose.

   If appending, use this format:

   ```
   ---

   <new prose>
   ```

   So that appended sessions are visually separable.
```

- [ ] **Step 6: Update the footer**

Replace:
```
9. **End with a footer:**

   ```
   ---
   Draft complete. <N> paragraphs, approximately <N> words.

   Bible entries auto-generated this session:
   - characters/<name>.md (Marcus)
   - (etc.)

   Recommended next steps:
   - Revise the draft. Aggressively.
   - Review the auto-generated bible entries and fill in gaps.
   - Run /authors-edit on the revised draft for a second pass.
   - Capture decisions in /authors-journal before you close the session.
   ```
```

With:
```
10. **End with a footer:**

   ```
   ---
   Draft complete. <N> paragraphs, approximately <N> words.
   Saved to: <resolved-target-path>

   Bible entries auto-generated this session:
   - characters/<name>.md (Marcus)
   - (etc.)

   Recommended next steps:
   - Open <resolved-target-path> and revise. Aggressively.
   - Review the auto-generated bible entries and fill in gaps.
   - Run /authors-edit on the revised draft for a second pass.
   - Capture decisions in /authors-journal before you close the session.
   ```
```

Replace:

```
- Drafts are written to stdout, NOT to a file. The user decides where prose lives.
```

With:

```
- Drafts are written to `manuscript/<current>` on disk FIRST, then displayed in chat. The user can open the file directly in an editor after the draft completes.
```

### Task 5: Update /authors-channel with explicit save trigger

**File:** `skills/authors-channel/SKILL.md`

When the channeled author generates prose, an explicit user command ("save that," "commit," "add to chapter") appends the last prose block to `manuscript/<current>`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Update the description**

Edit the frontmatter description. Replace:
```
description: Load a named author persona into the current conversation for direct collaborative drafting or editing. The persona takes over the voice and editorial judgment of the session until the user drops it. Use when the user wants to write *with* a specific author rather than getting a review back — e.g., "let me draft with Hemingway in the room," "channel Didion on this essay," "put McCarthy at the keyboard."
```

With:
```
description: Load a named author persona into the current conversation for direct collaborative drafting or editing. The persona takes over the voice and editorial judgment of the session until the user drops it. Generated prose stays in chat by default; saying "save that" or "commit to manuscript" appends the last prose block to manuscript/<current>.md. Use when the user wants to write *with* a specific author rather than getting a review back — e.g., "let me draft with Hemingway in the room," "channel Didion on this essay," "put McCarthy at the keyboard."
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Insert a new section before Notes explaining the save trigger**

Before the `## Notes` section, insert:

```markdown
## Saving prose to the manuscript

Generated prose stays in chat by default — the channel mode is collaborative, and not every exchange is manuscript-worthy (revisions, questions, craft discussion all happen here too).

When the user wants to capture a prose block, they say one of these:
- "save that"
- "commit"
- "add to chapter"
- "save to manuscript"
- "write that down"

When triggered:

1. Identify the "last prose block" — the most recent response where you generated substantive narrative prose (rough heuristic: >50 words of in-character narrative, not meta-discussion or a one-line revision).
2. Resolve the target path:
   - Read `.great-authors/project.md` if it exists. The `## Manuscript` section has `Current:` — use that filename under `<cwd>/manuscript/`.
   - If no Manuscript section exists or `Current:` is empty, ask: "Where should I save this? (default: `manuscript/chapter-01.md`)" — save the answer to project.md for next time.
   - If `manuscript/` doesn't exist, create it.
3. Append the prose block to the target file with a `---` separator if the file already has content.
4. Confirm in one line: `(Appended to manuscript/chapter-02.md — 234 words.)` — then continue in character.

**If the user asks to save multiple blocks at once** (e.g., "save the last three paragraphs"), comply. If they ask to save something NOT previously generated in this session, politely note that "save" works on prose you've already produced.

**Do not auto-save.** If the user doesn't explicitly trigger a save, prose stays in chat. This is intentional: channel mode is for co-writing, and not every exchange deserves to be in the manuscript.
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Update Notes**

Replace:
```
- Never reproduce an author's actual published work. Every persona's identity section includes this constraint.
```

With:
```
- Never reproduce an author's actual published work. Every persona's identity section includes this constraint.
- Save triggers are opt-in. If the user seems to want prose saved but doesn't use the trigger language, gently remind them: "Say 'save that' if you want me to drop the last block into `manuscript/<current>.md`."
```

```bash
  git add skills/authors-channel/ && \
  git commit -m "feat(authors-channel): add explicit save trigger for prose blocks"
```

### Task 7: Update README

- [ ] **Step 1: Update section header**

`## What's in v0.6` → `## What's in v0.7`

- [ ] **Step 2: Update the Per-project memory section**

Replace:
```
## Per-project memory

For novels, book-length nonfiction, or any project where you want consistency across sessions, run `/authors-project-init` in your project directory. It creates:

```
.great-authors/
├── project.md      # genre, voice, premise, POV, tense
├── voice.md        # voice rules for this project
├── timeline.md     # chronology
├── glossary.md     # invented terms, brands, dialect
├── characters/     # one file per character (use /authors-build-character)
├── places/         # one file per place
├── scenes/         # one file per scene or beat card (use /authors-build-scene)
└── journal/        # dated session entries (use /authors-journal)
```

Every author persona reads the relevant bible files — including the most recent journal entry — before editing any passage. No author "memorizes" the project; each invocation reads what's relevant, each time.
```

With:
```
## Per-project memory

For novels, book-length nonfiction, or any project where you want consistency across sessions, run `/authors-project-init` in your project directory. It creates two sibling directories:

**`.great-authors/`** — the project bible (metadata):
```
.great-authors/
├── project.md      # genre, voice, premise, POV, tense, manuscript config
├── voice.md        # voice rules for this project
├── timeline.md     # chronology
├── glossary.md     # invented terms, brands, dialect
├── characters/     # one file per character (use /authors-build-character)
├── places/         # one file per place (use /authors-build-place)
├── scenes/         # one file per scene or beat card (use /authors-build-scene)
└── journal/        # dated session entries (use /authors-journal)
```

**`manuscript/`** — the actual writing:
```
manuscript/
└── chapter-01.md   # (or whatever filename you chose at init)
```

Every author persona reads the relevant bible files — including the most recent journal entry — before editing any passage. Generated prose from `/authors-draft` lands in `manuscript/` automatically; prose from `/authors-channel` lands there when you say "save that."
```

---

## Self-review

- **Origin story:** field feedback from a live test. Addresses the specific gap the channeled author flagged ("prose stays in chat, that's a hole").
- **Placeholder scan:** clean.
- **Defaults honor the user's stated preferences:** prompt-once-and-persist for filename; explicit save trigger for `/authors-channel` (not auto-save).
- **Risk:** `/authors-draft` now mutates files on disk by default. If the user expected stdout-only behavior from v0.4, this is a breaking UX change. Mitigated by the announce step ("Saving to: manuscript/chapter-01.md") and the --to override.
- **Not done:** there's no CLI flag to opt OUT of saving in `/authors-draft`. If someone wants one-off generation without disk side effects, they can pass `--to /dev/null`. Good enough for v0.7; revisit if it becomes a real ask.
